title: Watermelon
description: Pete and Billy have a watermelon of w kilos. They want to divide it into two parts, each part weighing an even number of kilos. Determine if this is possible.
input_spec: The first line contains a single integer w (1 <= w <= 100) -- the weight of the watermelon.
output_spec: Print "YES" if it is possible to divide the watermelon as described, and "NO" otherwise.

routes.name: Parity analysis
routes.description: Determine divisibility by analyzing even/odd properties of the weight.

observations.title: Both parts must be even
observations.description: If we split w into a and b where a + b = w, both a and b must be even positive integers.

hints.nudge: What does it mean for a number to be 'even'?
hints.question: If both parts must be even, what can you say about their sum?
hints.formal: The sum of two even numbers is always even. So w must be even.

observations.title: w = 2 is the edge case
observations.description: w = 2 splits into 0 + 2, but 0 kilos is not a valid part (each part must weigh a positive even number).

hints.nudge: Are there any small values of w you should check carefully?
hints.question: If w = 2, what are the only two ways to split it? Are both parts positive?
hints.formal: w = 2 can only split as 0 + 2. Since 0 is not positive, w = 2 should output NO. The answer is YES iff w is even AND w > 2.

observations.title: Simple condition: w even and w > 2
observations.description: The complete solution is just checking w % 2 == 0 and w > 2.

hints.nudge: Can you express the solution as a single boolean condition?
hints.question: Combine the parity check with the edge case -- what's the simplest condition?
hints.formal: Print YES if w % 2 == 0 and w > 2, else NO.
